White or mirrored craft with gleaming black windows and fill the skies with a clear orangish green gas with pulsing blue lights on the underside.

The big twist is that the UFO we see in the trailer is a living, breathing “creature” that is hungry and feeds on the  people/horses on the land. 
There are no aliens that come out of the “ship”. It’s also more of a Sci-Fi Thriller with a lot of suspense.

A lot of ppl feel he doesn't make horror movies, like this is horror or "us" or "get out" and I just want to know what they think they are 😂. There is more than one type of horror and just bcuz you didn't get scared or as scared as you wanted to be doesn't mean anything either. You have slashers, jump scares, thrillers (which can be considered a different movie genre also), etc.. His to me are sort of artistic and unsettling. I'd say unsettling is how I'd describe the actual horror events in his work.
It's definitly a different kind of horror.  I don't actually have a word for it.  I can't really call it a slow burn because they are fully on fire by the end of the story.
The thriller genre has virtually nothing to do with horror. Unless you are specifically talking about the thriller subgenre “horror thriller”

Horror comedy is a distinct genre. There aren't a lot of examples out there because it's hard to pull off. Shawn of the Dead and Evil Dead are two that come to mind. They're all genuinely horrifying, but also hilarious.
@DrMbutu  uh.... yeah?

@weeeezzlll  yes, that too. Some ppl just throw in a bunch of random things and try to call that "comedy". As if extra blood is funny when it doesn't make sense? Some ppl can get it right and it's genuinely funny. Their comedic timing and horrific... horrifying? Idk lol - timing are good.

Spoilers below












Apparently it was cut content from test screenings where he was a superfan of gordy going to potentially shoot a castmember. He ended up being the one to shoot gordy. The crowd was the audience.

@vriskaxtereziotp ooooh that makes sense. Instead of that they just added police sirens and a gunshot in order to make it seem like the police killed Gordy. I wonder what peeles goal was with the superfan shooter

@falcon_5696 The majority of the film is a metaphor for fame/ the damages of it/and spectacles. Ricky saw the shoe instead of gordy (he was focusing on the wrong thing)
so I wouldn't be surprised if the superfan was to mirror people getting too caught up from things. 

It was cut because it didn't really flow well with the movie in the screen testing. 

Of course thats just something Ive seen multiple people who got to go to the test say.
@vriskaxtereziotp interesting. Thanks for explaining this to me! I’m sure some other people will appreciate it to. That superfan  character really stuck out to me when I first saw the trailers.

I watched Nope by Jordan Peele the day it came out at the first showing today, and I gotta say it's even worth a second watch, which I also did. This movie is a completely new take on "extraterrestrial life" and I loved it, the movie in itself was basically an experience and journey for the viewer. The music was also magnificent all in itself, I even found myself going on Spotify to look at the OST so I could find my favorite music from the movie. All around I must say this movie was 11/10 and I will be buying whichever stream it somes out on first and a extra behind the scenes thing if there would be one. I'll never forget my experience watching this Film, Thank You Jordan Peele.

Saw the movie today at a Regal in Orlando.  Overall average.  Takes a long time to get into it the plot.  The plot is very cheesy and almost sarcastic.    It seems like a low budget set (Says it cost 68M).   It basically is set in an abandoned , rural place with only 1 or 2 rooms used in a depicted old farm house, a few barns and an abandoned wild wild west tourist atttaction.  The good thing is that it is cast well.  There are alot of times the sound is muffled and I missed phrases and words.  Does not even compare to movies such as The   Village, War of the Worlds or Aliens.  Overall, Im glad I saw it, but it falls short of being a well put together film.  5/10.
